name: Mora, Los Yebenes, Consuegra y Madridejos – circular from Villacanas
name_original: Mora, Los Yébenes, Consuegra y Madridejos – circular desde Villacañas
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Villacañas traverse the distinctive landscapes of La Mancha, characterized by relatively flat to gently undulating plains. The region features significant natural attractions such as the Villacañas Lagoon Complex, a system of wetlands including Laguna Larga and Laguna de Peña Hueca Natural Reserve. Cyclists can also utilize "El Trenillo," a former railway line converted into a greenway, offering a paved surface through vineyards and natural areas. These routes provide varied terrain suitable for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Villacañas The most popular road cycling route is Lagunas y Albardinales del Cigüela – circular from Quintanar de la Orden, a 65.4 miles (105.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route explores the wetland areas and flat plains of the Cigüela region. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Mora, Los Yebenes, Consuegra y Madridejos – circular from Villacanas, a difficult 76.7 miles (123.5 km) path. This route offers a longer ride with more significant elevation gain, passing through several towns in the La Mancha area. Local road cyclists also love the La Mancha de Toledo – circular from Quintanar de la Orden via Lillo and Corral de Almaguer, a 44.8 miles (72.1 km) trail leading through the agricultural landscapes of La Mancha, often completed in about 2 hours 48 minutes. Road cycling around Villacañas is defined by expansive plains, wetland complexes, and greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides through natural reserves to more challenging routes connecting multiple towns. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villacañas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general terrain like for road cycling around Villacañas?
A: The Villacañas region, situated in the heart of La Mancha, offers predominantly flat to gently undulating plains. This characteristic terrain makes it suitable for road cycling of varying intensities, with well-maintained paths and expansive views across agricultural landscapes and wetlands.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Villacañas?
A: Yes, the region offers routes with minimal elevation gain, ideal for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31813391" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camuñas Ethnological Museum – Cervantes Sculpture loop from Villafranca de los Caballeros&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 16.2-mile (26.2 km) option with only 269 feet (82 m) of elevation gain.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while road cycling in Villacañas?
A: Road cycling routes often pass by the significant Villacañas Lagoon Complex, a system of wetlands including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5421216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Grande de Villafranca&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5878870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Peñahueca&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4529824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Larga of Villacañas&lt;/a&gt;. These areas are part of the 'Humedales de La Mancha' Site of Community Importance and are Special Protection Areas for Birds (ZEPA), offering serene natural scenery and opportunities for birdwatching.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, some routes connect to towns with notable landmarks. For instance, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023177323/mora-los-yebenes-consuegra-y-madridejos-circular-from-villacanas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mora, Los Yebenes, Consuegra y Madridejos – circular from Villacanas&lt;/a&gt; passes through several towns in the La Mancha area, allowing you to explore places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3161518" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Madridejos&lt;/a&gt; and potentially view the famous Consuegra windmills.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Villacañas?
A: The relatively flat terrain makes Villacañas enjoyable for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for longer rides. Summers can be hot, while winters are typically mild, though conditions can vary.

Q: Is there convenient parking for cyclists in Villacañas?
A: Villacañas, as a local town, typically offers various public parking options. While specific cyclist-dedicated parking isn't highlighted, general town parking should be available for those arriving by car with their bikes.

Q: Can I reach Villacañas with my bike using public transport?
A: Public transport options to Villacañas, such as regional buses or trains, may have specific policies regarding bicycle carriage. It's advisable to check with the respective transport provider (e.g., Renfe for trains, Interbus for coaches) directly for their current bike policies and any associated fees or restrictions before planning your journey.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Villacañas?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in the area are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023060951/lagunas-y-albardinales-del-cigueela-circular-from-quintanar-de-la-orden" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas y Albardinales del Cigüela – circular from Quintanar de la Orden&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the wetland areas and flat plains.

Q: What is 'El Trenillo' and how can I cycle on it?
A: 'El Trenillo' is a former railway line near Villacañas that has been converted into a greenway. This route offers a pleasant, paved cycling experience, passing by natural features like Laguna Larga and traversing vineyards. It's an excellent option for a smooth and scenic ride.

Q: What kind of challenge can I expect on road cycling routes in Villacañas?
A: The routes around Villacañas range from moderate to difficult. While many routes are relatively flat, some, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023177323/mora-los-yebenes-consuegra-y-madridejos-circular-from-villacanas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mora, Los Yebenes, Consuegra y Madridejos – circular from Villacanas&lt;/a&gt;, offer a longer ride of 76.7 miles (123.5 km) with a more significant elevation gain of 2,335 feet (712 m), providing a good challenge for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer distances, routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023060951/lagunas-y-albardinales-del-cigueela-circular-from-quintanar-de-la-orden" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas y Albardinales del Cigüela – circular from Quintanar de la Orden&lt;/a&gt; cover over 65 miles (105 km), offering an extended ride through the region's unique landscapes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Villacañas?
A: Road cyclists often praise the expansive plains, the unique wetland complexes, and the greenways like 'El Trenillo' for providing varied and enjoyable riding experiences. The region's network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides through natural reserves to more challenging routes connecting multiple towns.
tips:
The Town Hall is perhaps the most unique building in the municipality, representing a beautiful metaphor with its name and use.
Its architect was Ezequiel Martín Martín, and its construction began in 1927 and was completed practically a year later in 1928. The tile-clad tower was completed in 1930.
The Arch of San Huberto in Los Yébenes appears to be related to the Chapel of San Huberto, mentioned in local publications as part of the celebrations of the patron saint of hunters.

Cerro Calderico rises in the middle of the La Mancha plain, being visible for many kms. away and giving Consuegra (Toledo) its own stamp of identity. As the most relevant heritage elements we find the Castle and the Windmills that extend along its top following its longitudinal profile. Its views of the mills and castle, worth seeing.

waypoints.name: View of the Windmills and Castle of Consuegra
